title: Lastrea torresiana
taxonomy: Lastrea torresiana is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It belongs to the genus Lastrea, which is a type of evergreen fern. Lastrea torresiana is also classified as a member of the order Polypodiales, a large group of ferns with complex leaf arrangements. The species is native to certain regions and thrives in humid environments. Further classification may also include it as a member of the subfamily Acystopteroideae, within the Athyriaceae family.

intro: Lastrea torresiana, also known as the Torres' fern, is a species of fern in the genus Lastrea, characterized by its delicate fronds and lush green foliage. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea torresiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Lastrea torresiana is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or extract, and is used to treat respiratory issues, skin problems, and gastrointestinal disorders.
